response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack engineer, but his profile does not match this role's core requirements. His only professional C++ experience was a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments ending in 2015, far short of the mandatory 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++. He has no SQL Server or Oracle experience (MySQL/MongoDB only) and no fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain background — RIDE Capital was a retail investment fintech, not capital markets systems. His career trajectory (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, web platforms) points away from the Windows C++ financial-systems work this consulting engagement demands.
strengths: Deep general engineering seniority with strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of requests per second)
Some native-code background: C/C++ components at National Instruments and systems-level DLang work
concerns: Professional C++ experience totals under a year and ended in 2015 — nowhere near the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience, and no fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain exposure
Recent career is entirely web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js), suggesting a poor trajectory fit for a Windows C++ consulting role

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering fundamentals and CI/CD proficiency
